channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Trump officials say they can build 250-foot arch without Congress’s approval
item.description: To build a triumphal arch near Arlington Cemetery, the administration hopes to rely on a 100-year-old authorization for a different project.

item.title: The unintended consequences of mass deportations
item.description: The unintended consequences of mass deportations

item.title: White House resisted letting doctor with Ebola return to U.S.
item.description: The administration’s reluctance to bring an infected American back to U.S. soil marked a sharp contrast with the 2014 West Africa Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Former Cuban president Raúl Castro is indicted in U.S. on murder and conspiracy charges
item.description: The charges in the deadly downing of two jets in 1996, likely to remain symbolic, come as the Trump administration has ratcheted up pressure on Cuba.

item.title: Longest-serving D.C. schools chancellor to leave for education nonprofit
item.description: Lewis D. Ferebee, who was appointed in 2018 and oversaw the district’s post-pandemic recovery in math and reading scores, will become CEO of EdReports.

item.title: I caught my in-law’s ‘vegan’ partner eating a burger. Hax readers give advice.
item.description: After busting in-law’s preachy partner as pseudo-vegan, letter writer wants to burn special menus and serve him the ashes.

item.title: Why do diners get bad tables, even when it’s not busy? Ask our food critic your dining questions.
item.description: Washington Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Harvard faculty vote to limit A grades for undergraduates
item.description: The result announced Wednesday is the most prominent symbol of a reckoning at some elite schools concerned by the increasing number of A’s — a widespread issue that some faculty members warn is fundamentally damaging the integrity of education.

item.title: James Murdoch, son of media mogul Rupert Murdoch, buys New York magazine and news site Vox
item.description: Several other Vox titles are not included in the transaction and will make up a second, independent company under a new name.

item.title: Trump’s revenge in Kentucky could cost the GOP
item.description: Thomas Massie’s defeat sharpens a dangerous instinct before the midterms.

item.title: Barney Frank, influential congressman and gay rights hero, dies at 86
item.description: The Massachusetts Democrat led a sweeping overhaul of the financial industry after the Great Recession. He was one of the first openly gay congressmen in history.

item.title: Two hosts in today’s On the Record
item.description: Two hosts in today’s On the Record

item.title: Takeaways from primaries where Trump demonstrated power over GOP
item.description: It was a big night for the president as his favorites swept Republican foes in Georgia, Pennsylvania and Kentucky.

item.title: Primary results. Trump’s legal deal. War powers. San Diego shooting. El Niño. AI impacts. Exercise bursts. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Georgia Senate GOP primary heads to runoff where electability is a top issue
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins and ex-football coach Derek Dooley move on to a primary runoff in Georgia, with the winner facing Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff. The state's Senate race is among a handful that could determine party control next year.

item.title: Rep. Thomas Massie loses GOP primary in Kentucky, the latest casualty of Trump’s wrath
item.description: Massie lost to Trump-endorsed Ed Gallrein after repeatedly defying the president, the latest Republican unseated after crossing Trump.

item.title: What Stephen Colbert’s show ending means for the rest of late-night TV
item.description: “The Late Show,” an American television institution, goes off the air this week. It almost certainly portends changes to come for the classic television format.

item.title: Senate advances resolution to block further strikes on Iran but lacks clear path to force Trump to end the war
item.description: Four Republicans joined Democrats in voting to discharge a war powers resolution from committee. Seven similar resolutions since the start of the war had failed.

item.title: Virginia Gov. Spanberger vetoes bill to build legal marijuana market, displeasing fellow Democrats
item.description: Recreational marijuana is legal in Virginia, but the governor said the proposed regulations for retail sales weren't strong enough.

item.title: How Putin forced Poland to build a brutally ‘efficient’ wall
item.description: Eastern European countries are keeping out Russia’s “weaponized migrants.” Reporters got a look.

item.title: Trump's deal with IRS also bars agency from pursuing past tax claims against president, his family or his businesses
item.description: A one-page addendum to the deal, signed by acting attorney general Todd Blanche, was posted to the Justice Department’s website a day after officials announced an agreement with President Donald Trump that included the creation of a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund meant to compensate people who, like Trump, claim they have been targeted by a politicized justice system.

item.title: Scientists now say this worst-case climate scenario is ‘implausible.’ Here’s what retiring it would mean.
item.description: A U.N. panel on climate change seems poised to retire RCP 8.5, a scenario in which the world does nothing to curb planet-warming emissions, in its projections. Some on the right, including President Donald Trump, are now suggesting the move shows that global warming does not pose a significant threat. Many climate scientists, however, say the reality is more nuanced.

item.title: NAACP calls for college sports boycott in states targeting Black voting power
item.description: The civil rights group urges Black athletes to withhold commitments to athletic programs in eight states over redistricting, and it also asks fans to stop buying tickets.

item.title: Trump endorses Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton for Senate over GOP incumbent John Cornyn
item.description: The president’s political advisers and top Senate leadership in Washington had urged him to back Cornyn, arguing that Paxton has too much personal baggage and would be a weak general-election candidate in a once solidly red state that has become increasingly worrying for Republicans.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: An ailing husband wants the empathy he denies his spouse
item.description: Carolyn Hax: An ailing husband wants the empathy he denies his spouse

item.title: I saw America vilified in Iran. Then came the culture shock.
item.description: Many Americans are conflicted about their own country. It's still a beacon to the world.

item.title: A cargo ship’s crew tried to escape the Strait of Hormuz. They met with a hail of bullets.
item.description: The Filipino sailors had huddled on the vessel’s bridge to cast a final vote: Should they risk the perilous six-hour journey, made treacherous by mines and Iranian attacks? Although the path was closed to most international traffic, there was no way out but through.

item.title: 25 states sue Trump administration, saying health worker shortage will worsen with federal loan limit
item.description: The lawsuit comes after the Education Department finalized rules that reduce the amount of money graduate students can borrow from the federal government.

item.title: A warning in today’s On the Record
item.description: A warning in today’s On the Record

item.title: GOP Rep. Thomas Massie fights to survive Trump’s wrath in most expensive House primary ever
item.description: Few Republicans in Congress have clashed more bitterly with President Donald Trump over the past year than Massie, who has bucked the GOP on key votes and — against Trump’s wishes — led a bipartisan bill that forced the release of the government’s full files on the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Massie is now in the fight of his political life to beat back a Trump-endorsed challenger in Tuesday’s Kentucky primary.

item.title: Iran talks. San Diego shooting. Trump deal. Brush fire. Ebola outbreak. ‘Forever chemicals.’ Better sleep. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Head and neck cancers are on the rise. Here’s how to reduce your risk.
item.description: The good news: It’s entirely possible to take control of the major factors driving new cases of these cancers.

item.title: Why Congo’s Ebola outbreak is far more alarming than hantavirus
item.description: The world has been fixated on the wrong public health crisis, Leana S. Wen writes.

item.title: Five people, including two teenage suspects, now reported dead after shooting at Islamic Center of San Diego
item.description: The shooting is being investigated as a hate crime because of its location, police said, and there is no further threat.

item.title: One dead in shooting at San Diego Islamic Center, police said
item.description: The Islamic Center describes itself online as the largest mosque in San Diego, and it serves as both a religious institution and a school.

item.title: Trump says he has called off an attack on Iran after Middle East leaders asked him to give talks another try
item.description: In a Truth Social post, Trump said the leaders of Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates had asked him to “hold off” in light of “serious negotiations” on a peace deal.

item.title: ICE agent charged in shooting of immigrant during Minneapolis crackdown
item.description: County prosecutors issued a warrant for the arrest of the ICE agent, who faces felony assault charges in the nonfatal shooting. The victim was one of three people shot during January’s crackdown.

item.title: EPA wants to repeal limits on ‘forever chemicals’ in drinking water
item.description: If finalized, the proposal would end the Biden-era restrictions for four toxic PFAS compounds and give utilities two more years to comply with limits on two other compounds.

item.title: All of Musk's claims in lawsuit against OpenAI rejected in federal trial
item.description: In a win for OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, a judge dismissed all of Elon Musk’s claims against the artificial intelligence company after a jury found that Musk exceeded the statute of limitations for bringing his lawsuit against OpenAI.

item.title: Ebola outbreak prompts U.S. to screen some travelers at airports, other ports of entry
item.description: The U.S. is relying on a special public health authority to bar non-U.S. passport holders who have been to Uganda, Democratic Republic of Congo or South Sudan from entering the country.

item.title: Trump’s deal to drop suit against IRS creates $1.8B fund for those who claim they were targets of a ‘weaponized’ justice system
item.description: The deal, laid out in a document signed by acting attorney general Todd Blanche, grants a commission broad authority to distribute the money, drawn from taxpayer dollars.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Losing patience with a friend who is too complacent with his life
item.description: How do you tell a friend that he’s not being brave enough to turn his life around?

item.title: Trump’s unhealthy grip on the stock market is now in writing
item.description: The volatility he provokes is bad for investors and bad for America.

item.title: Swamped by data center demands, Dominion Energy just opted for a megamerger
item.description: NextEra Energy plans to buy the Virginia-based power company, creating the world’s largest regulated utility, as the U.S. races to keep up with AI power needs.

item.title: A lot of land in today’s On the Record
item.description: A lot of land in today’s On the Record

item.title: Trump agrees to drop his family’s $10 billion lawsuit against IRS; court filing does not mention details of any settlement
item.description: President Donald Trump will drop the lawsuit over the leak of tax filings to the media, his lawyers said in a court filing.

item.title: Prayer festival. White House. Job market. Ebola. Humidity map. Head and neck cancer. Ultramarathons. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Trump-backed prayer festival on National Mall draws thousands: ‘We welcome Jesus!’
item.description: Critics of Sunday's "Rededicate 250" event said it portrayed the United States inaccurately as a Christian nation and sought to erase the line between church and state.

item.title: These secret government investigations fly in the face of the Constitution
item.description: These secret government investigations fly in the face of the Constitution

item.title: The woman who keeps beating men at America’s most punishing running events
item.description: Fueled by mashed potatoes and moxie, Rachel Entrekin blew past the field at a 250-mile race known for breaking competitors’ will.
